name: Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta Trail
name_original: Acequias del Aceituno y Cecarta – circular desde Lanjarón
description: Hike the easy 3.8-mile Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta Trail in Lanjarón, enjoying historic acequias and stunning Sierra Nevada views.
region_intro: Hiking around Lanjarón offers access to the southern slopes of the Sierra Nevada National Park and the Alpujarras region. The terrain features dramatic gorges, verdant valleys, and a network of traditional Moorish irrigation channels known as *acequias*. Hikers can explore diverse landscapes, from lush pine and chestnut forests to olive groves, with many routes following the Río Lanjarón valley and its abundant water sources. Best hiking trails around Lanjarón The most popular hiking route is Lanjarón Circular Trail (PR-A 345), a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores diverse scenery and historic elements around the town. Another top favourite among local hikers is Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta Trail, an easy 3.8 miles (6.1 km) path. This trail follows an ancient irrigation channel, offering scenic views of the surrounding agricultural landscape. Local hikers also love the Barranco del Salado – circular from Lanjarón por La Peñuela, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail leading through the Salado ravine and past La Peñuela, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Lanjarón is defined by high mountain views of the Sierra Nevada, historic *acequias*, and the dramatic gorges of the Río Lanjarón valley. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging treks. The routes in Lanjarón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Lanjarón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Lanjarón?
A: Lanjarón offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with a good mix of easy, moderate, and challenging options to explore the Sierra Nevada foothills and Alpujarras region.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Lanjarón?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. The region is known for its gentle paths along ancient irrigation channels. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984074472/acequia-aceituno-cecarta-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.8-mile path offering scenic views of the agricultural landscape.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Lanjarón features many circular trails that allow you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984073620/lanjaron-circular-trail-pr-a-345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lanjarón Circular Trail (PR-A 345)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.9-mile route exploring diverse scenery and historic elements around the town. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984081545/barranco-del-salado-circular-from-lanjaron-por-la-penuela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Salado – circular from Lanjarón por La Peñuela&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.1-mile trail.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls or natural springs?
A: Lanjarón is famously known as the 'spa town' of Andalusia due to its abundant natural springs and medicinal waters. Many hiking routes follow the Río Lanjarón valley, featuring picturesque streams, waterfalls, and natural pools. The 'Ruta del Agua' (Water Route) is particularly renowned for winding along these natural water sources and old water mills.

Q: What kind of historical or natural landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in both natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the ruins of the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3515488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lanjarón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers magnificent views. Hikers will also encounter unique Moorish irrigation channels called *acequias*, and enjoy panoramic vistas of the Sierra Nevada, the Río Lanjarón Valley, and even the distant Mediterranean coast from various viewpoints like Mirador del Castillo and Mirador de la Cañona. For natural features, look out for the dramatic gorges and ravines, such as the Barranco del Río Lanjarón.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Lanjarón?
A: Lanjarón offers hiking opportunities year-round, but the best times are generally spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter offers the chance to see snow-capped peaks in the Sierra Nevada, though some higher trails may be inaccessible.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for more experienced hikers, Lanjarón provides challenging routes that delve into rugged terrain. The Lanjarón Gorge Hike is known for its adventurous character, towering rock formations, and pristine natural landscapes. For high-altitude trekking, tours to the mountain lakes of the upper Lanjarón valley are available, with some routes reaching above 3000m.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Lanjarón?
A: The hiking trails around Lanjarón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, the well-maintained paths, and the unique combination of mountain views, historic acequias, and abundant water features.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the traditional Moorish irrigation channels?
A: Yes, the traditional Moorish irrigation channels, known as *acequias*, are an integral part of the landscape and many trails run alongside them. These historic waterways offer scenic beauty and insight into ancient agricultural practices. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14599279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Placeta de Santa Ana – Aceituno Irrigation Channel loop from Lanjarón&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of a route that follows an acequia.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs visible from the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in Lanjarón offer sweeping vistas that include various reservoirs. You can enjoy views over Embalses de Rules and Beznar. Specifically, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/797938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Béznar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight that can be incorporated into your hike.

Q: Is it possible to access the high mountains of Sierra Nevada from Lanjarón?
A: Lanjarón serves as a gateway to the Sierra Nevada National Park. While many trails offer views of the high peaks, more challenging routes can take you into the upper Lanjarón valley, with opportunities for high-altitude trekking and exploring mountain lakes. These routes can reach above 3000m for those seeking a true high-mountain experience.
tips: This high mountain rural road is heavily traveled by farmers, ranchers, staff from the Sierra Nevada Protected Park and hikers. It begins in the Lanjarón Spa and reaches Fuente Calvo and the El Tello area.

The eras were spaces that were used for the threshing of cereals. The oldest ones are circular because animals were used to circle around them pulling the tools used to separate the grain from the rest of the plant. Something interesting about them is that they were for communal use and were located on communal land close to population centers.
The place is a beautiful picnic spot, unless one has to resort to the luxury of benches and tables. it lies between two canals, in the penumbra, and no hiker has passed there within 30 minutes.

faqs.question: Where does the Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta Trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently starts and ends in the charming town of Lanjarón, often near the town hall (Ayuntamiento). Parking is generally available within Lanjarón itself, making it easy to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta Trail is generally considered easy to moderate. While much of the route is manageable, there is a notable steep, zigzagging ascent near the beginning. After this initial climb, the path tends to level off, becoming easier. Some sections along the acequia can feel a little vertiginous, but metal fencing has been installed in these areas for safety.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse range of terrain, including dirt paths, paved sections, and rocky stairs. The trail closely follows the historic acequias (irrigation channels), so you'll often walk alongside or above flowing water. Be prepared for some uneven surfaces and the occasional rocky step.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a blend of natural beauty and historical charm. You'll walk along an Old cobblestone path, pass by The Threshing Floors of Lanjarón, and experience the ancient Acequia de Mezquerina Trail. Beyond these, enjoy stunning valley views, distant Sierra Nevada peaks, lush forests with centennial chestnut trees, and the picturesque old quarter of Lanjarón with its traditional 'tinaos' and mossy fountains.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in sections with steep drops or when passing through populated areas of Lanjarón. Please also ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly vibrant with rich colors. Early spring also offers a delightful experience as the area comes alive with bright blooms and the scent of orange blossoms. The constant sound of flowing water makes it pleasant even in warmer months, but always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Does this trail require any permits or have entrance fees?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta Trail. It is freely accessible to the public. As this trail is located within the broader Sierra Nevada National Park area, please always adhere to general park regulations regarding nature conservation and waste disposal.

faqs.question: Are there any good spots for a picnic or rest along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! There's a well-equipped picnic area at Huerta de las Monjas, featuring stone benches and ample shade from massive chestnut trees. It's a perfect spot to take a break, enjoy a snack, and soak in the tranquil surroundings.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other local trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Sendero Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta, Sendero Bordaila - Acequia Nueva, and Sendero Cerecillo - Mezquerina. It also briefly touches upon the Circular de Lanjaón and Sendero Pedro Calvo.

faqs.question: Is the Acequia Aceituno - Cecarta Trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to its manageable distance and beautiful surroundings, this trail is often recommended as a family-friendly walk. While there's an initial ascent and some sections with steep drops (which are fenced for safety), the overall experience is rewarding for most ages. Always supervise children, especially near the acequias.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: Prepare for truly picturesque scenery! You'll be treated to gorgeous views of the valley below, the majestic distant Sierra Nevada peaks, and from higher points, you might even catch a glimpse of the hazy Andalusian coastline. The trail also winds through charming village sections and lush groves.
